question.text: The grandfathering provision for capital gains on REIT units applies to units acquired on or before which date?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The grandfathering provision for capital gains on REIT (and InvIT) units applies to units acquired on or before 31st January 2018. This is the same cut-off date used for grandfathering of listed equity shares and equity mutual fund units when LTCG tax under Section 112A was introduced. **The Trap:** 31st March 2018 is the most common trap because students often confuse the grandfathering date with the financial year-end. The actual date is 31st January 2018 (Budget Day reference, when the LTCG tax was announced). **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund Distributors) tests the same grandfathering date for equity mutual funds; the date is consistent across equity shares, equity MFs, REITs, and InvITs. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For clients who acquired REIT units before 31st January 2018, calculate cost of acquisition as the higher of actual cost or the fair market value as on 31st January 2018 -- this can significantly reduce their LTCG liability.
